chapters.title: Understanding kids' minds eases tantrum management
chapters.text:
Parenting transforms established habits and routines dramatically. Everything changes when a new member joins the family. You will notice that some things and people do not excite you anymore, and you may find deep pleasure in spending time with your loved ones.

However, the lack of personal time and space can also affect your perspective. You may feel anxious and vulnerable. Furthermore, insufficient sleep and exhaustion can lead to disputes with your partner. Some studies say children are a reason for declining marital joy and gladness. Others suggest that marital satisfaction varies with children's ages : the older their kids get, the more time couples can enjoy together.

Maintaining intimacy with your partner is arduous when children are small. In 1957, E. LeMasters found that 83% of all new mothers and fathers experienced a “severe” crisis. Another research in 2009 studied 132 couples and found that about 90% faced a reduction in marital satisfaction after the birth of their first child. Thus, a baby can transform your life and your relationships. However, it is easier to cope with the changes if you have a shoulder to lean on.

Being a supportive and caring partner becomes more challenging when you have children.

Dividing your time impedes your productivity if you have a job or are running a business, but it’s something parents must manage. Parenting can be stressful and frustrating for both, and neglecting your responsibilities will put twice as much of a burden on your partner, leading to irritation and hurt.

Fathers generally spend less time with their children than mothers if they are the main breadwinners. However, it does not disqualify or reduce the father’s role in the family. It just means that women are at the more challenging end in childcare while fathers struggle to support their families financially and mentally. Raising children is more manageable when relatives can help you out. Still, most people prefer to live far from their parent’s homes, as constantly dealing with others’ opinions and lifestyles can be frustrating.

Parents must support each other throughout the journey, starting from pregnancy, not at delivery. Couples must come up with a parenting plan for the first time. How will they split the chores ? Will one of the parents work or stay home for the first few years? Answering these and other parenting-related questions will help a couple prepare for the journey they are about to start.

chapters.title: Understanding kids' minds eases tantrum management
chapters.text:
Many people don’t realize how drastically their life will change until their first child is born. Of all the problems new parents face, sleeplessness is the most common. It’s one thing to miss out on sleep because of watching The Game of Thrones and another to wake up every ten minutes to the sound of baby cries. The situation grows complicated when you lack sufficient sleep for months or years. Therefore, a small but constant sleep insufficiency may dramatically affect your behavior, physical and mental health, and performance.

Newborns and toddlers can often get on a parent's nerves by demanding what they want now. Persuading toddlers to buy the toy they want tomorrow won’t stop their tantrums because they cannot picture the future in their minds. They want things at once because they live in the now.

Children's perception of time differs from adults because the prefrontal cortex doesn't fully develop until age 25. This brain part is located behind the forehead, allowing humans to organize their thoughts and actions. It is barely formed in small children, so kids struggle to remain focused or plan their activities. Therefore, dressing a child for daycare can feel like an epic battle, especially if they are running late.

There’s the parenting life of our fantasies, and there’s the parenting life of our banal, on-the-ground realities. ~ Jennifer Senior

Moreover, children are curious and adventurous; they rarely know the right things to do or steps to take, yet they want to move fast. Hence, parents must always be one step ahead for protection and support.

Understanding a child's development stages will make parenthood a more peaceful and joyful experience.

While adults always stress the importance of living in the present, they rarely appreciate their ability to predict the future. You can determine your schedule or learn from past mistakes. On the contrary, toddlers will protest and cry if you tell them to collect their toys and go to sleep because they don’t understand they can play with them again tomorrow.

Therefore, trying to explain the logic of time to your child is useless. You can repeat “tomorrow” or “next week,” but these words have no meaning for the child. The best strategy would be to show patience and gently redirect their attention.

Thus, knowing the principal difference between an adult's and a child’s brain is critical in saving you time, strength, and nerves.

chapters.title: Splitting chores alleviates parenting
chapters.text:
Parenting transforms established habits and routines dramatically. Everything changes when a new member joins the family. You will notice that some things and people do not excite you anymore, and you may find deep pleasure in spending time with your loved ones.

However, the lack of personal time and space can also affect your perspective. You may feel anxious and vulnerable. chapters.title: Children’s lifestyles can be frustrating for adults
chapters.text:
Children learn about the world through seeing, doing, touching, and experiencing; adults, on the other hand, understand the world through the information they receive from books and the media. Therefore, many adults forget what it feels like to be an explorer with stained clothes or splashing in puddles. Nonetheless, it’s an essential part of growth for a toddler as they learn through movement and feelings.

Another challenge a toddler’s parents face is the flexibility and diversity of their child’s schedule. Young kids switch from running to playing to crying in a minute. Such a lifestyle can feel highly stressful for people accustomed to habits, predetermined tasks, and measured productivity.

Furthermore, combining childcare and housework can deteriorate the parent’s emotional state as they cannot leave the child while cleaning or cooking. Therefore, the only way is to let them take part. You can show your kid how to use a broom or allow them to play with flour while you are making dough. Such activities are perfect for learning and bonding.

Moreover, you must let your child experience this world independently as they discover through play. Activities like running, jumping, and climbing help children gain control over their bodies; falling and little scratches teach them to be more cautious next time. They also form personality traits like perseverance, bravery, and openness that may affect their life in the future. Hence, it’s critical to let children make mistakes, fall, and learn through experience; otherwise, they will not complete this stage successfully.

Playing and doing crafts with young children will improve their development.

Children's brains constantly develop as they observe and experience new things. Unlike adults, philosophy is what children do naturally. The awkward questions can make parents blush, laugh, or feel confused, especially when asked in public. Nonetheless, children’s curiosity allows them to develop exponentially. Thus, don’t let ethics or personal limitations stop you from enjoying this rewarding stage with your child.

Finally, children won’t stay toddlers forever, so make it your priority to invest in your kid. For example, teach them chess for strategic thinking, bake cakes and do chores to incorporate them into household routines, and enroll them in classes and sports to learn teamwork, perseverance, and resilience.

Did you know? According to an IMARC report, the global toy market reached 170.7 billion dollars in 2022 and will make 267.9 billion by 2028.

chapters.title: Work-life balance is essential when you have kids
chapters.text:
Parenting doesn’t always equal happiness because factors like marital status, living conditions, and work constantly affect parents and children. For example, few will say that bringing up a kid while managing two jobs and going through a divorce is an exciting and joyful experience.

You must be a contented adult to be a happy parent.

The context of single parenting becomes more spread with divorce and separation. Single mothers experience more economic hardships and health challenges resulting from parenting. Many show signs of social withdrawal as they are busy making ends meet while caring for their children.

Men experience undue pressure just as much as women, especially regarding their financial situation. In many families, women are responsible for the children and household while men go to work to pay. Reports by the American workforce show that today's fathers spend more hours at work than their counterparts who do not have kids. Most working fathers struggle to manage their work-life balance. The expenses grow with the child, pushing a family breadwinner to work harder. This pressure forces some men to work on the weekends or take two jobs missing precious moments with their kids. The environment and family roles also predetermine a child’s well-being. Children rarely see their parents when both of them work. Moreover, they grow up in smaller families, have private rooms, and live in suburbs and exurbs, where everyone minds their business. Thus, they may feel alone.

Parent-child time is about quality, not quantity.

The older a child gets, the more isolated they may feel; however, requiring their parents’ attention will get tougher as they reach puberty — they’ll shift attention to their peers, sometimes neglecting their family completely. Therefore, the stronger the parents-children connection is, the more likely your child will come to you with possible challenges or issues they may face.

Managing work and family life is burdensome but rewarding. Therefore, make your child your top priority. You can still enroll them in the classes but spend at least an hour of quality time daily. It will show your child that you love and care for them.

Did you know? In 1999, Ellen Galinsky found out that 85% of Americans believe parents don't spend enough time with their children, but only 26% of kids wish to spend more time with their parents.

chapters.title: Adolescents require different love and support than toddlers
chapters.text:
For most, having a baby is alluring and exciting, while helping a child through puberty sounds complex and burdensome. Most adults remember how it felt to be a teenager with mood swings and anxiety. People often avoid thinking about this parenthood part because they fear it. They don’t know how to manage it correctly because the line between leading and controlling may be as thin as an adolescent’s patience. Moreover, proper support and communication during this period may be decisive in their future, while neglecting or controlling your adolescent kid may result in hostility and conflicts.

According to Wendy and Laurence Steinberg in Crossing Paths, 40% of parents experience a decline in their mental health once their firstborn enters adolescence. Сonstant feelings of rejection, low sense of self-worth, and declined sex lives may increase physical symptoms of distress, like headaches, insomnia, and stomach aches. Thus, you may suffer from insomnia and anxiety even when your child is almost a grownup.

Limiting a teenager’s independence may lead to rebellion.

Adolescents are like the human equivalent of salt; they usually intensify whatever mix they're in, but all the same, adolescents need to be protected. Nurturing is a parent's way of life, but children crave independence and a sense of purpose as they age. Therefore, control and excessive protection may lead to adverse results like overreaction, emotionality, hostility, or blaming. This stage must be about listening to your child’s needs and desires and trying to understand them.

Letting go is a critical part of educating an adolescent.

In adolescence, children's lives become complex and messy; they face peer pressure, learn resilience, struggle to understand their emotions triggered by hormones, and determine their values and views. It is a tremendous shift in life that will require parents’ time and patience. Ironically, this is when parents and children switch places regarding their attention: now children answer “Maybe later” or “I’m too busy” to their parent’s offers to watch a movie or play a board game.

They don’t tell you, when you become a parent, that the hardest part is way, way down the road. ~ Dani Shapiro

Overall, parenting at the childhood stage is about developing who your child will be; during adolescence, it's about responding to who they want to be. Thus, parents must prepare for it and be ready to listen and support when the time comes.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Parenting is a costly and rewarding activity. Its hardships pay off when your baby laughs, starts to walk, or says their first words. However, raising a child is challenging because it completely distorts your schedule, habits, priorities, and rest and can substantially affect your mental and physical health.

Furthermore, maintaining healthy and caring relationships with a partner becomes more complex with the constant attention your baby craves. Therefore, parenting is most effective when it is shared. Sharing responsibilities is critical for a healthy and happy family. For example, stay-at-home mothers require some “me time” from a child not to feel overwhelmed and anxious, while fathers who work must spend some time with their kids after work; otherwise, the child will lack balanced attention from both parents. Modern child-rearing has become more arduous because people lead excluded and busy lives with no extended family to support them. Some parents have even accepted that having babies equal s losing your freedom. Nonetheless, the situation changes with a kid’s age: the older they get, the more space they need. So, don’t get discouraged; eventually, most parents find time and energy for their relationships, work, or hobbies.

Finally, ensure that you are ready for children before you have them, including emotional, physical, and financial availability. Parenting is a life-long journey with struggles, adventures, victories, and failures; you will never know how to do everything, but you will learn.

Try this

Select and write down the most problematic aspects of parenting or those you fear. It can be dealing with tantrums or spending quality time with your kid after work. Ask your friends or relatives how they managed it, or find real-life examples and situations online. Parents can learn a lot from each other. Therefore, stay open and ask for help when you need it.
